text.title: Monorepo Workspace Structure
text.summary: Turborepo workspace layout with apps/, packages/, and root configuration for JS/TS monorepos.

rule.statement: Separate apps (runnable services) from packages (shared libraries) at the workspace root.
rule.rationale: Apps are app-shaped (no exports field); packages are library-shaped (with exports). Mixing them confuses Turborepo's dependency graph and publishing workflows.

example.code: monorepo/ ├── turbo.json ├── package.json # root: scripts delegate to turbo ├── pnpm-workspace.yaml ├── apps/ │ ├── web/ # Next.js app — no exports, private: true │ └── api/ # Express/Fastify service — no exports └── packages/ ├── ui/ # shared React components — has exports map ├── config/ # eslint, tsconfig presets — versionless ├── types/ # shared TS types — has exports map └── utils/ # shared utilities — has exports map

rule.statement: Use pnpm with a pinned version in the root package.json `packageManager` field and add an `engines.pnpm` constraint.
rule.rationale: pnpm has the best workspace protocol support (`workspace:*`). Pinning prevents version drift across machines and CI. npm workspace hoisting causes phantom dependency bugs.

rule.statement: Keep config packages (eslint preset, tsconfig) at version 0.0.0 with private: true. Never publish them.
rule.rationale: Config packages are build-time tools consumed via `workspace:*`. Versioning them creates unnecessary churn; publishing them leaks internal configs.

rule.statement: Use `workspace:*` for internal dependencies in pnpm. Never use relative paths (`../../packages/ui/src`) — always `@org/ui`.
rule.rationale: Relative imports cross app/package boundaries and break when packages are moved or published. `@org/ui` via `workspace:*` resolves correctly in all contexts.
